title: CloudFront Origins, OAC, SSE-KMS, and Private Delivery · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss decisions for cloudfront origins, oac, sse-kms, and private delivery and accepts only bounded behavior, causal changed-case evidence, and an explicit AWS transfer gate. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for private CloudFront-to-S3 origin contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish regular S3 origins from website endpoints by HTTPS, OAC, redirects, error pages, methods, and public-access requirements.
Configure OAC with always-sign where appropriate and define viewer protocol, origin protocol, request signing, and forwarded Authorization behavior.
Scope the CloudFront service principal to the exact distribution SourceArn and required object actions without public or cross-distribution access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Oai Oac Migration
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 2435 during a private media release. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; keep browser execution deterministic and move AWS claims to an authorized transfer gate; then change cache and retrieval cost assumptions and defend the new budget result. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Oai Oac Migration.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for migrate oai to oac with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Oai Oac Migration, keep Oai Oac Migration connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

steps.title: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Oai Oac Migration
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Oai Oac Migration: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz Go file-delivery team handles case 1198 during a high-traffic evidence intake window. Build deterministic Go evidence to validate a regular S3 OAC origin with exact distribution and KMS authorization; retain stable tenant and file identity without exposing it in metric labels; then change the object version and prove stale authority is rejected. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. AWS SDK packages, networks, S3, CloudFront, KMS, IAM, WAF, databases, credentials, host state, race and leak checks, load, faults, restore, billing, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.

Evidence target: S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Oai Oac Migration.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For S3, CloudFront, and Go Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Oai Oac Migration, keep Oai Oac Migration connected to executable Go storage and delivery decision evidence.

content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Migrate OAI to OAC with temporary dual authorization, deployed distribution evidence, negative tests, monitoring, and policy rollback.
